leagueBriefing: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander tied a historic Wilt Chamberlain record in a thrilling win over Denver, while James Harden became just the ninth player in NBA history to cross the 29,000-point threshold.

games.headline: Harden hits 29,000 career points as Cavaliers cruise past depleted 76ers
games.brief: James Harden carved out another piece of basketball history on Monday night, becoming the ninth player ever to reach 29,000 career points. The milestone came during a comfortable win for Cleveland, who took full advantage of a Philadelphia squad missing its four best players. With Joel Embiid and Tyrese Maxey among the sidelined, the 76ers lacked the firepower to keep up with a Cavaliers team that has now won 18 of its last 24 games and looks increasingly dangerous in the Eastern Conference race.
bullets: James Harden finished with 21 points, bringing his career total to 29,017 points across 17 seasons.
Keon Ellis provided a major spark off the bench for Cleveland, hitting four 3-pointers and finishing with 19 points.
The 76ers were without Tyrese Maxey, Joel Embiid, Paul George, and VJ Edgecombe, who account for nearly 87 points per game.
Cleveland has been dominant at home recently, going 8-1 at the Rocket Mortgage FieldHouse since late January.
The Cavaliers swept the four-game season series against Philadelphia, outscoring them by a total of 497 points.
games.context: Cleveland moves within one game of New York for the third seed in the East, while Philadelphia slides to eighth.

games.headline: Sharpe and Agbaji lead Nets past short-handed Grizzlies for second straight win
games.brief: The Brooklyn Nets are starting to find a bit of a rhythm, securing their second consecutive win by handling a Memphis team that was down to just eight available players. Even with leading scorer Michael Porter Jr. resting, Brooklyn’s depth took over, fueled by a dominant bench performance. The Grizzlies fought hard behind a career night from Rayan Rupert, but they simply ran out of gas in the fourth quarter as the Nets pulled away to find some late-season momentum.
bullets: Day'Ron Sharpe tied his season high with 19 points to lead six Nets players in double figures.
Brooklyn’s bench was the difference-maker, outscoring the Memphis reserves 67-40.
Rayan Rupert scored a career-high 20 points for a Grizzlies team missing ten regular contributors.
The win marks the first time Brooklyn has won consecutive games since snapping a 10-game losing streak on Saturday.
Memphis has now lost four straight games and 11 of their last 14 contests.
games.context: Brooklyn continues to play spoiler after snapping a long skid, while Memphis struggles to remain competitive amidst a massive injury crisis.

games.headline: Gilgeous-Alexander ties Wilt Chamberlain record and sinks Nuggets with late dagger
games.brief: In a heavyweight battle that lived up to the billing, Shai Gilgeous-Alexander put on a masterclass to lead Oklahoma City over Denver. SGA didn't just hit the game-winning step-back three; he also tied Wilt Chamberlain’s legendary record of 126 consecutive 20-point games. Despite a massive triple-double from Nikola Jokic and a wild sequence that saw Denver tie the game with 8 seconds left, Gilgeous-Alexander proved why he’s in the MVP conversation by silencing the Nuggets at the buzzer.
bullets: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander recorded 35 points and a career-high 15 assists with zero turnovers.
SGA's 126-game streak of 20+ points ties the all-time record set by Wilt Chamberlain from 1961-63.
Nikola Jokic put up 32 points, 14 rebounds, and 13 assists for his 24th triple-double of the season.
The Thunder are undefeated (6-0) since Gilgeous-Alexander returned from an abdominal injury.
Ajay Mitchell contributed 24 points for OKC in his first game back after a 20-game absence.
games.context: The Thunder solidify their status as a Western Conference power while SGA etches his name alongside one of the greatest records in NBA history.

games.headline: Jazz rookies stun Curry-less Warriors in chaotic late-game thriller
games.brief: The Utah Jazz may be struggling lately, but they found a hero in two-way player Blake Hinson, whose late 3-pointer buried the Warriors in Salt Lake City. Golden State entered the night severely shorthanded without Stephen Curry, Kristaps Porzingis, or Al Horford, and despite a gritty 22-point effort from De’Anthony Melton, they couldn't overcome their own mistakes at the free-throw line. In a game featuring 19 lead changes, Utah’s youngsters showed more poise down the stretch to steal a win.
bullets: Blake Hinson hit the go-ahead 3-pointer with 29.9 seconds remaining to secure the upset.
Rookie Kyle Filipowski dominated the glass with 19 points and 15 rebounds.
The Warriors struggled at the stripe, shooting just 14-of-21 compared to Utah’s nearly perfect 27-of-29.
Seth Curry made his season debut after missing 40 games with back issues, contributing 13 points.
Utah snapped an eight-game losing streak with the victory.
games.context: A devastating loss for Golden State as they struggle to maintain their footing in the tight Western Conference Play-In race.

games.headline: Clippers hit .500 mark as Leonard and Mathurin outduel Towns in Inglewood
games.brief: The Los Angeles Clippers have officially erased a disastrous 6-21 start to the season, climbing back to .500 with an impressive win over the Knicks. Kawhi Leonard continued his incredibly consistent run, but the story of the night was Bennedict Mathurin, who scorched New York for 22 points in the second half alone. Karl-Anthony Towns did everything he could for the Knicks with 35 points, but the Clippers' late-game execution and balanced scoring proved too much to handle at the Intuit Dome.
bullets: Kawhi Leonard scored 29 points, marking his 42nd consecutive game with at least 20 points.
Bennedict Mathurin erupted for 28 points off the bench, including 22 in a dominant second-half performance.
Karl-Anthony Towns had 35 points on 13-of-17 shooting but fouled out in the closing seconds.
The Clippers have won five of their last six games to move to 32-32 on the season.
New York has now lost three of their last four games and hasn't won in LA since 2022.
games.context: The Clippers continue their massive mid-season surge toward the Play-In tournament, while the Knicks lose ground in the race for home-court advantage.
